DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT SOCIAL VOCATIONAL SERVICES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT AND MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT - DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED, WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL TERMINATION IN VIOLATION OF GOV. CODE 12940 IF PLAINTIFF CAN PLEAD IN GOOD FAITH A COGNIZABLE CLAIM NOT ALREADY CONTAINED IN THE REMAINING DISCRIMINATION AND TERMINATION CAUSES OF ACTION. THERE IS NO COGNIZABLE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL TERMINATION IN VIOLATION OF GOV. CODE 12940. OVERRULED AS TO THE 10TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT HIRING, SUPERVISION, AND RETENTION, THE 11TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE, AND THE 13TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. FOR THE 10TH, 11TH AND 13TH CAUSES OF ACTION, THE EXCLUSIVITY RULE DOES NOT BAR ACTIONS WHERE THE MISCONDUCT EXCEEDS THE NORMAL RISKS OF THE EMPLOYMENT RELATIONSHIP. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS. HINE V. DITTRICH (1991) 228 CAL.APP.3D 59, 64 IS DISTINGUISHABLE. IN HINE, THE PLAINTIFF "SPECIFICALLY ALLEGE[D] HE SUFFERED NO INJURY UNTIL HE WAS DISCHARGED." (HINE V. DITTRICH (1991) 228 CAL.APP.3D 59, 64.) HERE, PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THAT SHE SUFFERED INJURY BEFORE SHE WAS DISCHARGED. FOR THE ELEVENTH AND THIRTEENTH CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS AND MAY PLEAD AN ALTERNATE THEORY OF NEGLIGENCE LIABILITY UNDER A SEPARATE HEADING. DENIED AS TO THE MOTION TO STRIKE THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. PLAINTIFF HAS ADEQUATELY PLED FACTS TO SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. AS PLED, DEFENDANT EMPLOYEE'S HARASSMENT AND DISCRIMINATION FORM THE BASIS OF OPPRESSION AND MALICE, AND DEFENDANT ALLEGEDLY RATIFIED THE ACTS BY TERMINATING PLAINTIFF. ORDER SIGNED WITH THE MOVING PAPERS. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: ANGIE DINER, CSR # 9581

Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Apr-23-2014 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-02-2014 Set For Trial On Dec-01-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Apr-16-2014 Reset From Apr-16-14 To Apr-23-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-28-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT SOCIAL VOCATIONAL SERVICES, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT AND MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT - DEMURRER is sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the seventh cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of Gov. Code 12940 if Plaintiff can plead in good faith a cognizable claim not already contained in the remaining discrimination and termination causes of action. There is no cognizable cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of Gov. Code 12940. Overruled as to the 10th cause of action for negligent hiring, supervision, and retention, the 11th cause of action for negligence, and the 13th cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress. For the 10th, 11th and 13th causes of action, the exclusivity rule does not bar actions where the misconduct exceeds the normal risks of the employment relationship. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts. Hine v. Dittrich (1991) 228 Cal.App.3d 59, 64 is distinguishable. In Hine, the plaintiff "specifically allege[d] he suffered no injury until he was discharged." (Hine v. Dittrich (1991) 228 Cal.App.3d 59, 64.) Here, Plaintiff alleges that she suffered injury before she was discharged. For the eleventh and thirteenth causes of action, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts and may plead an alternate theory of negligence liability under a separate heading.
Denied as to the motion to strike the prayer for punitive damages. Plaintiff has adequately pled facts to support a claim for punitive damages. As pled, Defendant employee's harassment and discrimination form the basis of oppression and malice, and Defendant allegedly ratified the acts by terminating Plaintiff. ORDER SIGNED WITH THE MOVING PAPERS. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Angie Diner, CSR # 9581 =302/PJB
